decision: Register time-series momentum separately from MOM-01 using matched 30-7 and 90-7 own-return signals; require spot long/cash and point-in-time shortable perpetual cells, raw and lagged-volatility-managed exposure, daily mark-to-market, funding, margin and liquidation accounting.
rationale: Sprint 002 requires TS/CS separation; a separate identity prevents a broad momentum label from hiding implementation differences.

decision: Select inference by estimand; freeze bootstrap block or HAC lag rules, clusters, multiplicity family, minimum economic effect and target capital before execution. Retain Holm for confirmatory FWER and SPA/reality-check methods for strategy families.
evidence_refs: Sprint 001 validation protocol; external review triage
rationale: Multiple estimators answer different questions and do not constitute independent replications.

decision: Add lagged volatility, aggregate funding, beta and externally dated structural-break reporting cells. Use regimes as diagnostics, not model-selection permission; reject ex-post worst-period labels as confirmatory evidence.
evidence_refs: Sprint 001 Research Charter H8 market-state control; Sprint 001 robustness matrix
rationale: Ex-ante state definitions test stability without opening a regime-model search.

decision: Report target-capital multiples and break-even capacity after approved impact calibration; do not impose a universal fixed-dollar grid.
rationale: Capacity is strategy-, venue- and period-specific; arbitrary AUM cells cannot substitute for calibration.

decision: Require quantitative source audits and reviewer-frozen thresholds before unblocking; retain zero-tolerance point-in-time, vintage, mapping and lineage gates.
rationale: Thresholds must be objective but cannot be invented without measured source behavior.

decision: Refresh literature every six months from the prior cutoff or earlier after a material evidence/data-market event; append sources and decisions without rewriting prior sprints.
rationale: A fixed maximum interval prevents stale priors while trigger-based refresh handles structural change.

decision: Retain one sealed prospective holdout per declared research version; reject rolling opened windows as a replacement.
rationale: Repeated opening creates adaptive feedback and multiplicity rather than free statistical power.

decision: Do not register a joint momentum/carry experiment until the relevant standalone momentum and carry mechanisms independently pass data, cost and evidence gates.
rationale: Standalone attribution is required before interaction or composite claims.
